hypothesis: Incorporating stereochemical diversity metrics (e.g., chiral center enumeration, atropisomeric variation, and E/Z isomer tracking) as an additional constraint layer in NaviDiv will reveal that reinforcement learning optimization for property-targeted molecular generation disproportionately collapses stereochemical diversity faster than structural fingerprint-based diversity, and that adding explicit stereochemical penalty functions will recover a measurable improvement in the discovery of high-performing enantiomeric or diastereomeric candidates that are missed by current fragment- and scaffold-based constraints alone.
